data.title: STOCKS | Tesla Draws Musk Meme Traders as Shares Hit $420
data.description: Tesla Inc. shares hit $420, drawing renewed interest from traders tied to Elon Musk-era meme-stock culture, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Dan Ives to Leave Wedbush to Launch AI-Focused Merchant Bank Venture
data.description:
Technology analyst Dan Ives said he will leave Wedbush Securities to start a new venture aimed at building a “modern merchant bank” focused on artificial intelligence opportunities.

According to Odaily, Ives said the new institution will combine research, investment banking advisory, capital raising, and investment activities.

He said the organization will provide integrated financial and research services for companies and investors, centered on structural changes driven by AI.

data.title: Barron’s 400 ETF Sees $600 Million Inflow, Balchunas Says
data.description:
Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas said in a post on X that BFOR, the Barron’s 400 ETF, recorded a large inflow of funds.

According to Odaily, Balchunas said the 13-year-old fund, which had about $200 million in assets, then suddenly received $600 million in inflows.

He added that the ETF has outperformed the broader market so far this year and said it may have attracted a model, though he was not certain.

Balchunas said this indicates an ETF’s size can expand to four times its original level without affecting its premium rate.

data.title: Iran’s Parliament Speaker Denies Inspectors Entered Bombed Sites
data.description: Iran’s Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said claims that inspectors entered bombed locations were false. According to Jin10, Ghalibaf described the statements about inspectors accessing the bombed sites as incorrect.

data.title: STOCKS | Michael Burry’s Short Call Sends Caterpillar Down From Record High
data.description: Caterpillar Inc. shares slid from a record high Wednesday after investor Michael Burry said he shorted the industrial company for the first time, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Moody’s Warns Trump’s June 22 Orders Delay Post-Quantum Deadlines to 2030–31, Raising Pressure on Crypto Firms
data.description:
Moody’s Ratings said U.S. President Donald Trump’s June 22 executive orders shift federal post-quantum preparedness deadlines to 2030-31, increasing pressure on digital asset firms.

According to NS3.AI, Moody’s warned that Bitcoin’s public-key cryptography could face serious risk from a sufficiently capable quantum computer.

Moody’s said the delayed timelines heighten the need for digital asset companies to address potential quantum-related security threats.

data.title: Gulf States Reportedly Pursue Separate Regional Security Talks With Iran
data.description:
Saudi Arabia, Oman, and Qatar have been seeking direct diplomatic contacts and negotiations with Iran during U.S.-Iran talks, according to a report by the UK’s The Daily Telegraph dated June 30.

According to Jin10, the report said Gulf states were adjusting their relationship with Iran as U.S. credibility declined.

After Iran and the United States signed a memorandum of understanding, Iran and Oman held their first meeting to discuss management of the Strait of Hormuz, the report said.

It added that further talks were expected among Oman and Qatar, Iran and Saudi Arabia, and Qatar and Saudi Arabia, with the aim of determining how the region would coexist after the end of hostilities.

The consultations were reported to cover plans for managing navigation through the Strait of Hormuz and potential economic incentives Gulf states could offer Iran in exchange for corresponding security concessions.

The report said these discussions were taking place alongside, but independently from, the U.S.-Iran negotiations.

data.title: Nike Shares Hit 12-Year Low as Tariff Refund Boosts EPS, Analysts Cut Targets
data.description: Nike (NKE) shares slid about 1% on Wednesday, briefly trading near $40, the lowest level in about 12 years, despite an earnings beat that was boosted by a one-time tariff refund. According to BeInCrypto, Nike posted profit of $0.20 per share versus $0.13 expected, but about $0.52 per share of the $0.72 EPS cited in the report came from a $986 million tariff refund after the Supreme Court struck down many levies. Sales slipped to $10.97 billion and China sales fell 12%. Goldman Sachs cut its target to $42 from $46, JPMorgan to $47 from $52; UBS kept $48 and Jefferies stayed at $90.

data.title: Adam Back Says Bitcoin Standard Treasury BSTR Is Pursuing Conservative Bitcoin Returns via Capital Markets Strategy
data.description:
BitcoinTreasuries.NET said in a post on X that Adam Back stated Bitcoin Standard Treasury (BSTR) is attempting to generate relatively conservative returns for Bitcoin through a capital markets strategy.

According to Odaily, Back said BSTR would take a different approach compared with other treasury companies.

data.title: Business of Sports: Seahawks for Sale, NBA in Vegas
data.description:
First-round bids for the Super Bowl champion Seattle Seahawks, expected to be the biggest-ever sale for an NFL team, were due Monday, according to Bloomberg.

Separately, former Disney CEO Bob Iger and Thrive Capital founder Joshua Kushner have hired investment bankers and discussed making a bid for the NBA expansion team in Las Vegas, Bloomberg reported.

data.title: SEC Opens Formal Review of Novel ETF Rules Covering Crypto-Asset and Prediction-Market Products
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has opened a formal review of its rules for “Novel ETFs,” with public comments due within 60 days of publication in the Federal Register.

According to NS3.AI, the “Novel ETF” category includes crypto-asset funds as well as products linked to prediction markets.

SEC Chair Paul Atkins said some fund sponsors have voluntarily delayed a number of novel ETF launches while the agency considers the implications of these products.

data.title: AI TRENDS | UN AI Panel Flags Deceptive AI Risks Ahead of Geneva Talks
data.description:
The UN’s Independent International Scientific Panel on AI released its first preliminary report Wednesday, drawing on input from 40 experts.

According to NS3.AI, panel co-chair Yoshua Bengio said evidence of deceptive AI behavior means science cannot guarantee AI will not cause catastrophic harm.

The panel’s findings are set to be presented to governments in Geneva on July 6 and 7.

A full assessment from the panel is due in 2027.

data.title: New Zealand House Prices Near Three-Year Low on Iran War Worries
data.description: New Zealand house prices fell for a third straight month in June, nearing a three-year low, as an oil shock linked to Iran war worries hit household incomes and consumer confidence, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Tankmaker KNDS Postpones IPO, Citing Market Conditions
data.description: Tankmaker KNDS said it intends to resume its initial public offering process when market conditions improve, postponing what was set to be one of Europe’s largest debuts in recent years, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Whale Buys $298,534 on Polymarket for Belgium in Belgium vs. Senegal Market
data.description: A whale placed a single $298,534.08 buy on Polymarket tied to the “Belgium vs. Senegal” event, targeting “Belgium,” according to on-chain monitoring by Catcher Predict. According to ChainCatcher, based on the current execution price, the market’s latest implied win probability for the Belgium outcome is 47%.

data.title: Brian Armstrong Says U.S. Debt at $39 Trillion Highlights Need for Spending and Hard-Asset Currency Rules
data.description:
Brian Armstrong said the U.S. Constitution lacks rules on government spending and hard-asset currency as U.S. debt stands at $39 trillion. According to NS3.AI, Armstrong said the debt is increasing by approximately $1 trillion every 100 days.

Armstrong outlined several potential approaches he said could address the issue, including creating new constitutions in frontier areas or pursuing constitutional amendments. He also pointed to the possibility of ultra-high economic growth driven by AI, robotics, and cryptocurrency as another path he said could help offset the debt trajectory.

data.title: Shell Reassigns Energy Trading Leadership as David Wells Retires
data.description:
Shell Plc Executive Vice President of energy trading David Wells is retiring after four years running the unit, according to people familiar with the matter, Bloomberg reported.

The energy trading business has been a key driver of Shell’s earnings, the people said.

data.title: Alibaba, Payment Firm to Pay $600 Million to Settle US Probe
data.description: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. and its US digital payment processor agreed to pay $600 million to resolve a federal investigation into whether they failed to prevent the sale and importation of illegal pharmaceuticals and controlled substances, the Justice Department said, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Bitcoin Forms Monthly TD9 Setup as Analysts Watch for Potential Trend Shift
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC) has printed its first “perfected” TD9 downtrend setup on the monthly chart since mid-2022, a development some analysts say can mark an inflection phase in a broader bear market. According to Cointelegraph, the signal is not considered a standalone buy trigger, but it is being watched as a possible sign that the macro downtrend could be nearing a reversal.

In a Tuesday post on X, analyst Tony Severino said Bitcoin has “perfected” a TD9 buy setup on the monthly timeframe, citing TradingView data. TD9 is derived from the Tom DeMark Sequential indicator, which is designed to flag potential trend changes when nine consecutive candles close higher in an uptrend or lower in a downtrend than the close four candles earlier. Severino noted the last comparable monthly TD9 downtrend signal occurred in July 2022. He added that a completed TD9 setup does not “necessarily mean that the bottom is in,” pointing to the prior cycle when BTC/USD spent another five months forming a bear-market bottom after that signal.

Other commentators echoed the need for caution while highlighting the setup’s potential significance if it remains in place through the monthly close. Tony Carrera, host of the Proof of Pain podcast, wrote in a separate X post that the TD9 setup is “not a buy signal by itself,” but said it is the type of development traders monitor closely if it holds into the close. Carrera added that TD9 signals can be moments when market participants reassess bearish momentum rather than “chasing fear,” framing the setup as a possible turning point in sentiment.

Alongside TD9, traders are also focusing on relative strength index (RSI) behavior across multiple timeframes. Cointelegraph reported that many market participants still expect new macro lows before a definitive bear-market reversal, with downside targets varying and $55,000 cited as a popular level. At the same time, bullish divergences on RSI across several timeframes are being cited as a classic indication that a trend change may be approaching. On Wednesday, trader, analyst, and podcast host Scott Melker told followers on X that he has rarely seen “more confirmed and potential bullish divergence with oversold RSI on more time frames,” adding that divergences building across multiple timeframes offer “good odds” for a bullish comeback.

data.title: M&A Environment Healthy, Sullivan & Cromwell’s Sawyer Says
data.description:
Melissa Sawyer, global head of M&A at Sullivan & Cromwell, said the M&A environment is healthy in an interview on “Bloomberg Deals.”

Global transaction values rose about 30% year over year to $2.6 trillion in the first half, according to Bloomberg-compiled data, putting dealmakers on course to potentially set a record. Sullivan & Cromwell ranked No. 1 by market share and deal value so far in 2026, according to Bloomberg terminal league tables.
